rulings.Case Title: ANGIE AQUINO VS. MICHAEL MELKONIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 3, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF ANGIE AQUINO'S Motion For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 128.7(c)(1). DEFENDANTS, THEREFORE, WERE NOT ACCORDED THEIR FULL 21 DAYS TO WITHDRAW THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO OBTAIN AN ORDER SHORTENING THE 21 DAY PERIOD UNDER CCP 128.7(c)(1) OR TO ASK THAT THE MOTION TO VACATE BE CONTINUED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: AARON MARTINEZ VS. TAKE-TWO INTERACTIVE SOFTWARE, INC., A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 3, 2011, line 3, PLAINTIFF AARON MARTINEZ DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO THE ANSWER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT EACH OF ITS ENUMERATED DEFENSES IN THE ANSWER. DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ELISEO TURCIOS et al VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Answers To Deposition Questions And Appendix Of Non-California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 3, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Answers To Deposition Questions IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THE QUESTION REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S IMMIGRATION STATUS IS RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE OF FUTURE WAGES. RODRIGUEZ V. KLINE (1986) 186 CAL.APP.3D 1145. THE 2002 AMENDMENTS WERE ENACTED TO ADDRESS HOFFMAN PLASTIC COMPOUNDS, INC. WHICH DEALT WITH AN ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT'S ABILITY TO OBTAIN HIS/HER BACKWAGES WITHOUT FEAR OF SEEKING REDRESS BECAUSE OF THEIR IMMIGRATION STATUS. MOREOVER, THE DISCOVERY STANDARD IS BROADER THAN THE ADMISSIBILITY STANDARD UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 352 WHICH CAN BE RAISED AT TRIAL. ANY QUESTIONING OF PLAINTIFF ON HIS IMMIGRATION STATUS MUST BE NARROWLY TAILORED TO THE ISSUE OF FUTURE WAGES AND NOTHING FURTHER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION VS. DONALD WILLIAM DANMEIER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Evidence Sanction For Misuse Of Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 3, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANTS DONALD DANMEIER, DANMEIER ARCHITECTS, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORAION, MATTHEW KLUNIS Motion For Evidence Sanction For Misuse Of Discovery. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS PREMATURE AND THEY HAVE NOT MADE A SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF'S ACTIONS REGARDING THE PRIVILEGE LOG RISES TO THE LEVEL OF A "MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS". THE PARTIES SHOULD CONTINUE TO MEET AND CONFER TO ESTABLISH A DEADLINE FOR DETERMINING WHAT DOCUMENTS WILL BE PRODUCED OR BE DEFINITELY EXCLUDED (PLAINTIFF HAS ALREADY STATED THAT IT WOULD NOT SEEK ADMISSION OF PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS) AND WHEN THEY WILL BE PRODUCED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM J. KAHN VS. BETTY J. HEDGEMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 3, 2011, line 11, DEFENDANT BETTY HEDGEMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACTS TO SUPPORT THE QUIET TITLE CLAIM. HYATT V. COLKINS (1917) 174 CAL.580, 581-582. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE POSSESSION AND EJECTMENT IS A REMEDY TO QUIET TITLE. SOUTH SHORE LAND CO. V. PETERSON (1964) 226 CAL.APP.2D 725, 741. =(302/LMG)
